content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from ICM and RND frameworks for autonomous agents) can be repurposed as a prioritization gate for active learning agent memory, where only unexpected, high-curiosity experiences that trigger intrinsic reward are encoded into long-term memory, rather than storing every interaction the agent has. This creates a self-organizing memory system that automatically prioritizes information that improves future task generalization without manual memory pruning.
